notes: No state paid family leave. Federal FMLA may apply (12 unpaid weeks).
California PFL pays 70-90% of wages for 8 weeks of bonding leave. SDI covers pregnancy disability (typically 6-8 weeks for vaginal delivery / 8-10 for C-section).
Colorado FAMLI launched 2024. Up to 12 weeks paid family leave + 4 weeks for pregnancy/childbirth complications. Wage replacement up to 90% of low-wage workers.
12 weeks of paid family leave; 2 additional weeks for serious health condition during pregnancy.
Delaware Paid Family and Medical Leave begins benefit payments January 2026.
12 weeks paid family leave for new parents.
No state paid family leave. State employees only have a limited program.
Hawaii has Temporary Disability Insurance (covers pregnancy/recovery) but no paid bonding leave yet.
Illinois requires 5 paid sick days per year (2024+). Chicago has a paid leave ordinance.
Maine Paid Family and Medical Leave begins benefit payments May 2026.
Maryland Family and Medical Leave Insurance begins payments July 2026.
12 weeks paid family leave + up to 20 weeks medical leave (combined 26 max).
Minnesota Paid Leave benefit payments begin January 2026. Up to 20 weeks combined family + medical.
Voluntary state program; opt-in coverage available through MetLife.
12 weeks of paid family leave at 85% of wages; separate Temporary Disability Insurance covers pregnancy recovery.
12 weeks paid family leave at 67% of average weekly wage. Disability Benefits Law also covers pregnancy recovery.
State employees have 8 weeks paid parental leave; no statewide private-sector program.
Up to 12 weeks paid (14 with pregnancy complications). 100% wage replacement for low-wage workers.
Temporary Caregiver Insurance: 7 weeks paid bonding leave. TDI covers pregnancy disability separately.
Voluntary state PFL through The Hartford launched 2023; opt-in coverage.
No state paid family leave. State employees have 8 weeks paid parental leave.
12 weeks of paid family leave (up to 16 combined with medical). Up to 18 weeks with pregnancy complications.
